sanskrit: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च। वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम्॥
transliteration: sarvasya cāhaṃ hṛdi sanniviṣṭo mattaḥ smṛtir jñānam apohanaṃ ca | vedaiś ca sarvair aham eva vedyo vedānta-kṛd veda-vid eva cāham
literal_meaning: Of all / and / I / in the heart / seated / from Me / memory / knowledge / and forgetfulness / and / by the Vedas / also / all / I / indeed / am to be known / the maker of Vedānta / the knower of the Vedas / indeed / and / I
contextual_meaning: The deepest layer of mind is not personal; it is a trans-personal field that simultaneously supplies memory, knowledge, and the capacity to forget.  All psychological content—thoughts, insights, amnesias—emerge from this field, not from an isolated ego.  Recognizing this dissolves the illusion of a private mind and reveals that every search for understanding is already guided by the same intelligence.
psychological_state: Post-Insight integration: the felt-sense that ‘my’ thoughts are local weather on a shared sky, leading to relaxed humility and creative flow.
trigger_scenario: A coder debugs an algorithm at 3 a.m.; the solution ‘suddenly’ appears.  Instead of claiming genius, she senses the idea arrived from a larger field and whispers, ‘Thank you,’ feeling both grateful and transparent.
anchor_text: Your mind is a universal field remembering and forgetting through you.
